records.ety: [OF. fourage, F. fourrage, fr. forre, fuerre, fodder, straw, F. feurre, fr. LL. foderum, fodrum, of German or Scand, origin; cf. OHG. fuotar, G. futter. See Fodder food, and cf. Foray.]

defs: The act of foraging; search for provisions, etc.
quotes: He [the lion] from forage will incline to play.
One way a band select from forage drives A herd of beeves, fair oxen and fair kine.

defs: Food of any kind for animals, especially for horses and cattle, as grass, pasture, hay, corn, oats.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Foraged ; p. pr. & vb. n. Foraging (?).]

defs: To wander or rove in search of food; to collect food, esp. forage, for horses and cattle by feeding on or stripping the country; to ravage; to feed on spoil.
quotes: His most mighty father on a hill Stood smiling to behold his lion's whelp Forage in blood of French nobility.

defs: To strip of provisions; to supply with forage; as, to forage steeds.
